ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Teh O Mint; 'tea beverage' (FOODON_03301103) is the closest parent category covering plain brewed black tea beverages.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Tea, infusion) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for Teh O Mint or mint-flavoured plain tea.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for Teh O Mint as a specific food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup (approximately 240 ml / 240 g) of Teh O Mint as typically served at a Malaysian or Singaporean kopitiam or hawker stall.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA SR Legacy Item 14355) and Spearmint, fresh (USDA SR Legacy Item 02065) — combined estimation for plain black tea with mint infusion
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Teh O Mint is plain black tea (no sugar, no milk) infused with fresh mint leaves. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy entries for brewed black tea (FDC ID 14355) and fresh spearmint (FDC ID 172231). The mint contribution at typical infusion quantities (2–4 g fresh mint per 240 ml) is negligible; values are dominated by the plain brewed tea profile. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD for this specific beverage. USDA SR Legacy is used as the highest-priority approved secondary source for this generic beverage.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — plain tea and non-caloric beverages reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea without sugar has an effectively zero glycemic index as it contains negligible digestible carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables classify unsweetened tea as a non-glycemic beverage. Per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy numeric field requirements; gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is effectively 0. The mint infusion does not alter the glycemic profile.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (approximately 7 mg per 240 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Mint without added sugar is an excellent low-sodium beverage choice for individuals with hypertension; the sodium content of approximately 7 mg per standard cup is clinically insignificant, and the polyphenols in black tea may confer modest vasodilatory benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and zero sugar when served without added sweetener
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh O Mint served without sugar (kosong) contributes essentially no glycemic load and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients should confirm no sugar is added at point of service, as sweetened versions would significantly alter glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh O Mint is free of saturated fat and cholesterol and may be consumed freely by individuals with cardiovascular disease; the flavonoids in black tea have been associated with modest reductions in LDL oxidation in epidemiological studies, though clinical benefit from a single serving is limited.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Mint is a Malaysian and Singaporean beverage consisting of plain black tea (Teh O, meaning tea without milk) brewed or steeped with fresh mint leaves, served hot or occasionally iced. 'Teh O' is the Hokkien-derived kopitiam term for black tea without milk or condensed milk, distinguishing it from 'Teh' (tea with condensed milk) and 'Teh C' (tea with evaporated milk). The addition of mint (pudina in Malay) gives the drink a refreshing, slightly cooling aromatic character while preserving the mildly astringent, earthy flavour profile of the black tea base. It is commonly ordered without sugar (kosong) or with a small amount of sugar depending on personal preference. Nutritionally, when served unsweetened, Teh O Mint is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate. It is naturally free of dairy, gluten, and common allergens, making it suitable for vegetarian, vegan, and halal diets. The beverage has a very low glycemic index and glycemic load, making it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome when consumed without added sugar. The polyphenols and flavonoids present in black tea may offer modest antioxidant and cardiovascular benefits. Teh O Mint is widely available at kopitiams, hawker centres, and Malaysian-style cafes across Singapore and Malaysia, and is priced affordably as an everyday beverage.
common_user_queries: Is Teh O Mint suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Teh O Mint?
Is Teh O Mint the same as mint tea?
Can I drink Teh O Mint if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between Teh O and Teh?
Is Teh O Mint good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Teh O Mint exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for plain brewed black tea (the dominant component) with negligible mint contribution. The estimation is well-supported and the beverage profile is nutritionally simple, justifying medium rather than low confidence.
notes: Teh O Mint is a plain black tea beverage with mint; it contains no milk and no sugar when ordered kosong. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed plain black tea contains no measurable protein at typical brewing concentrations. fat_g is naturally zero as this is a non-dairy, non-fat beverage by nature. sugar_g is confirmed zero for the unsweetened (kosong) preparation; sweetened variants would differ. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a brewed liquid tea beverage. energy_kcal of 1.0 kcal per 100 g reflects the trace carbohydrate from tea solids. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate per serving is negligible. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for brewed tea. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. The cuisine_tags 'Noodle' and 'Dosa' from the input seed record are not applicable to this beverage and have been discarded. This record covers the unsweetened (kosong) preparation; a separate record would be required for sweetened variants.

improvement_actions_taken: Added mandatory multilingual local_names for Malaysian/Singaporean cuisine: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Estimated nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy for plain brewed black tea as no exact entry exists in HPB or MyFCD.
Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: glycemic_index set to 1, gi_category set to 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g, fat_g, sugar_g, and dietary_fibre_g.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported justification.
Populated all three required health_context conditions with clinical guidance.
